steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a line crosses the y-axis, which occurs when $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the y-coordinate from the graph
steps.content: Looking at where the line intersects the y-axis on the graph, the crossing point is at $y = 8$. The y-intercept is the point $(0, 8)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
